jdbc_runtime_components: config/DatabaseConfig.java creates and initializes the SQLite DataSource/schema
config/DatabaseInitListener.java introspects SQLite tables, columns, indexes and triggers
utils/DatabaseChecker.java checks the database through JdbcTemplate

required_indexes: unb (current schema non-unique; service lookups expect single result)
xianyuAccountId (make unique if one-cookie-per-account remains invariant)
createTime TTL candidate if retention applies

query_update_semantics: Preserve zero-based LIMIT/OFFSET paging and descending create/message/order time ordering; Spring Pageable is page-based and needs explicit offset adaptation where callers pass raw offsets.
Order search joins goods title and searches title, skuName, buyerUserName and content with SQL LIKE; Mongo needs aggregation/$lookup or denormalized goodsTitle plus escaped case policy.

Keyword fuzzy matching is message LIKE '%' || stored keyword || '%'; a normal Mongo regex query cannot directly express every-document keyword containment efficiently. Implement bounded candidate retrieval or a dedicated matching strategy.
Daily dashboard counts currently use SQLite date(...,'localtime') over string timestamps. Store BSON dates and calculate Asia/Shanghai day boundaries explicitly.
Revenue currently casts totalPrice String to REAL. Convert to numeric/Decimal128 or preserve a carefully tested conversion aggregation.
Auto-delivery no-SKU uniqueness normalizes null and empty skuId to the same key. Use a normalized field or partial unique indexes; plain compound unique indexing changes null/missing behavior.
Kami markUsed/markUnused are compare-and-set updates on status. Preserve as findAndModify/update-first with status predicate and inspect modifiedCount.
Human intervention active lookup and cleanup compare string datetimes to SQLite localtime. Replace with BSON Date and TTL/manual expiration query.
BaseMapper selectOne assumptions require uniqueness for username, settingKey, account cookie, xyGoodId and account/goods configs; Mongo repositories must not silently accept duplicates.
Bulk replacement of SKUs/properties deletes all by xyGoodsId then inserts ordered rows; retain atomicity or use embedded arrays/versioned replacement.

usage_inventory.wrapper_usage: LambdaQueryWrapper/LambdaUpdateWrapper occurs across 23 production files, including controllers and backup handlers; migration cannot be isolated to mapper interfaces.
backup_usage: AutoReplyBackupHandler uses JdbcTemplate and explicit delete/insert ordering

risky_transaction_areas: AccountServiceImpl.deleteAccountAndRelatedData manually deletes approximately ten account-owned domains before deleting the account. Mongo requires a replica-set transaction or idempotent cascade orchestration; verify every new collection is included.
Account save/update couples XianyuAccount and XianyuCookie. Preserve all-or-nothing behavior and add an account-cookie uniqueness invariant.

Kami acquisition uses a JVM ReentrantLock plus conditional markUsed, then usage records/config counts elsewhere. JVM locks do not protect multiple instances; use atomic findAndModify and a transaction for item, usage record and counters.

KamiConfigService catches exceptions inside @Transactional methods and returns failure objects, which can allow partial commits because no exception escapes. Correct rollback semantics during migration.
Goods SKU/property save performs delete-then-insert replacement. Multi-document Mongo transactions need replica-set deployment; embedding is an alternative if document size is bounded.
KeywordReplyService deletes contents then rule and creates rule then contents without @Transactional. Partial rule/content state is currently possible and should be fixed or embedded.
Order flows frequently execute select-then-insert/update and update state then confirm state without a transaction. Enforce unique account/order and account/pnm keys and use atomic upserts.
Backup restore performs multi-record upserts/deletes and cross-entity natural-key remapping. Define per-module transaction/idempotency and deterministic ID/reference remapping.
Batch goods save catches per-item exceptions inside a transactional outer method, so partial success is intentional. Preserve that product behavior rather than accidentally rolling back the batch.
schema_discrepancies: XianyuOrder/XianyuOrderMapper reference xianyu_order, but schema.sql does not create that table.

XianyuOrderMapper joins xianyu_goods_auto_delivery_record, but no matching entity, mapper or CREATE TABLE exists; the active order API uses XianyuGoodsOrderMapper instead, so confirm whether XianyuOrder is dead code before creating a collection.
Several entities store timestamps as String while others use Long or LocalDateTime; normalize BSON date representation without changing JSON/API serialization.
XianyuGoodsAutoDeliveryConfig.kamiConfigIds is serialized text; migrate to a typed list of references/IDs.
The schema has SQL update-time triggers. Mongo auditing callbacks must replace trigger behavior.
actionable_findings: Define repositories by domain, but reserve MongoTemplate custom implementations for order aggregation/search, dashboard date/revenue aggregation, keyword matching, raw-offset paging and atomic kami acquisition.
Use stable Long/String business IDs initially to avoid changing APIs and references; add a dedicated Mongo _id mapping strategy rather than exposing ObjectId changes.
Create compound/partial unique indexes before enabling writes, especially message, auto-reply, goods-config, auto-delivery-config, goods-order, SKU/property, keyword-rule and kami-usage invariants.
Require replica-set MongoDB if cross-document transactions are retained; otherwise redesign tightly coupled data (rule+contents, goods+SKUs/properties) as embedded documents.
Resolve the duplicate order models and missing xianyu_order/auto_delivery_record schema before implementation so dead SQL is not migrated as a second order source of truth.
Move persistence out of controllers and backup handlers into domain services/repositories so transaction and index semantics have one implementation path.
